prompt:
Update the design docs in this repo (the-valley) to reflect an evolved understanding of the project. This is a DESIGN-ONLY repo: prose in markdown under design/ plus README.md. There is NO code and you must NOT add any. Before writing anything, read README.md and ALL of design/*.md (contribute.md, verification.md, integration.md, integrator-internals.md, feedback.md, knowledge.md, scenarios.md, openquestions.md) and skim the git log, so you fully absorb the existing terse, declarative house voice (em-dashes, 'Origin:' pointers, no fluff). Match that voice exactly. This change is ADDITIVE + REFRAMING — do not delete existing content or break existing cross-references.

OVERARCHING REFRAME (apply throughout): the-valley is fundamentally a *recursive, transparent outcome-production engine*. Outcomes chain and recurse on a DAG — e.g. 'add code to a VCS' is an outcome that is part of 'deliver a user-loved feature to prod'. The substrate is GENERAL (open to general automation; even a non-code outcome like 'book concert tickets' is conceivable), but **the software development lifecycle (SDLC) is the v1 reference implementation**, not the definition. Keep the existing GitHub-unbundling material as the SDLC reference-implementation lens; layer the general framing above it. Make this explicit in README.md.

There are THREE threads. Treat #1 and #2 as SETTLED — integrate them confidently. Treat #3 as UNRESOLVED — add it but mark it clearly as needing further design; do NOT present it as decided.

=== THREAD 1 (SETTLED): The outcome DAG as a generative scheduler ===
Create a new doc design/outcomes.md. Key points:
- The dependency DAG is largely ALREADY LATENT in the knowledge graph (design/knowledge.md): the 'blocked_by'/'blocks' edges form a dependency DAG, and 'closes' + the attestation-success -> 'node-status-changed' event is exactly how an outcome finalizes a node. The new move is to read this graph GENERATIVELY (a production graph the system has pressure to complete), not just descriptively.
- Add a PRIORITY concept: root outcomes carry a priority that PROPAGATES down the DAG to their ancestors-of-completion. Under contention, the system schedules the FRONTIER nodes (unblocked nodes on the critical path to the highest-priority root). Producing the nodes that finalize the DAG for a high-priority root IS the scheduling mechanism. This is dependency-aware / critical-path scheduling, not an attention router.
- This SPLITS the single 'Priority layer architecture' open question (currently openquestions.md, under 'Attention, routing, and threads') into two distinct problems: (a) WORK SCHEDULING — now answered by DAG priority-propagation + frontier dispatch; (b) ATTENTION ROUTING — which finished/blocked outcomes a human must see — still open. Update openquestions.md accordingly: mark the work-scheduling half as resolved (pointing to outcomes.md) and keep the attention-routing half open.
- Dispatch is 'klaus-shaped' (a scheduler controller reads the frontier and dispatches agents against frontier nodes) — consistent with the existing open-question note 'events spawn new agent runs... klaus-shaped'.
- This FLIPS the existing open decision (openquestions.md 'Knowledge graph specifics' -> 'task may collapse into bug'): 'task'/outcome must STAY DISTINCT from 'bug'. The outcome/task node is the CENTRAL GENERATIVE node type (the unit of work production); 'bug' is merely one kind of problem. Update design/knowledge.md to (i) note the generative reading of blocked_by/blocks + closes, and (ii) reflect that task/outcome is a first-class, central type that does not collapse into bug. Consider whether the 'task' node type should be named/framed as 'outcome'; propose and apply what reads best with the existing node-type set.

=== THREAD 2 (SETTLED): Group / instance / federation ===
Create a new doc design/federation.md. Key points:
- Introduce an organizational layer ABOVE 'repo'. The basic unit of federation is the GROUP (a team, a company, whatever). Each INSTANCE is 1-to-1 with a group, and a group's instance runs the bus (NATS JetStream) + integrator + git hosting for that group.
- An instance is instantiable on a SINGLE MACHINE but runnable as a DISTRIBUTED system — the same design scales down and up.
- This reframes existing cross-repo open questions (cross-repo integration, cross-repo feedback, cross-repo nodes — currently under 'Cross-repo coordination') as INTRA-GROUP (within one instance) vs INTER-GROUP (federation across instances). Update those open questions to point at federation.md for the framing.
- Trust scores are scoped within a group; federation governs how trust and events cross group boundaries. The 'Discovery' open question is scoped by group/federation — update it to note this.
- Update README.md to mention federation-by-group as the distribution/identity model.

=== THREAD 3 (UNRESOLVED — mark as needs-work): Recursive self-transparency invariant ===
- Surface a CANDIDATE top-level invariant: 'No actor can durably change the system OR an output without transparency' — recursive all the way down. The system's own policy, controllers, and config are themselves outcomes governed exactly like code (the-valley builds the-valley).
- This currently exists only SCATTERED across openquestions.md (integrator self-integration; policy bootstrap; per-repo integrator config as a knowledge-graph 'config' node) and knowledge.md (principles load-bearing on integration). Add a short stub doc design/self-transparency.md that names the candidate invariant, lists those scattered facets as instances of it, and is CLEARLY MARKED as DRAFT / unresolved / needs design — do not resolve it. Add a corresponding open-question entry noting that these scattered items are facets of one unresolved invariant.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- README.md reflects the general outcome-engine thesis with SDLC as the reference implementation, and mentions federation-by-group.
- New docs: design/outcomes.md (Thread 1), design/federation.md (Thread 2), design/self-transparency.md (Thread 3, marked DRAFT/needs-work).
- design/knowledge.md updated for the generative edge reading and the task/outcome-vs-bug decision flip.
- design/openquestions.md updated consistently: priority question split (work-scheduling resolved, attention-routing open); task-vs-bug decision flipped; cross-repo + discovery questions reframed under federation; self-transparency added as an unresolved consolidated item. Preserve the existing 'Origin:' pointer style and add origin pointers to the new docs.
- All internal cross-references (doc links, [[id]] hints, openquestions origin pointers) resolve — no dangling references to files or sections that don't exist.
- Voice matches existing docs. No code added. This is purely design prose.

Open the PR with a body that summarizes the three threads and explicitly flags Thread 3 as unresolved/needs-further-design so the reviewer knows it is intentionally a stub.
